description: Early blight is a common fungal disease of potato that primarily affects older foliage and can also cause tuber lesions. Severe infection can lead to premature defoliation and reduced yield.
symptoms: Small dark brown to black spots initially appear on older or lower leaves
Leaf lesions enlarge and often develop concentric rings that create a target-like appearance
Yellowing may develop around infected areas
Multiple lesions may merge and cause leaves to dry and die
Severe infection can cause premature defoliation
Tubers may develop dark, sunken, dry lesions with underlying corky tissue

favorable_conditions.leaf_wetness: Repeated or prolonged leaf wetness favors infection
favorable_conditions.rainfall: Rain and irrigation can support spore dispersal and infection
favorable_conditions.additional_conditions: Disease is often more severe on stressed, nutrient-deficient, or senescing plants

spread: Rain splash and irrigation water can disperse spores
The pathogen can survive in infected crop debris
Volunteer potato plants and infected plant material can contribute to inoculum
risk_factors: Continuous potato or susceptible solanaceous cropping
prevention: Use healthy and certified seed tubers
Remove volunteer potato plants where practical
Provide adequate irrigation without unnecessarily wetting foliage
Monitor older leaves regularly for early symptoms

immediate_actions: Inspect the crop to determine the extent of infection
Remove heavily infected foliage in small plantings where practical
cultural_control: Reduce plant stress through appropriate irrigation
Manage volunteer potato plants and infected crop residues
biological_control: Consider locally registered biological fungicides labeled for Alternaria diseases where available

chemical_control.recommended: Where disease risk warrants treatment, use fungicides specifically registered for early blight on potato and follow a resistance-management program.
chemical_control.warning: Availability, registration, resistance restrictions, dosage, application interval, and pre-harvest interval vary by location and product. Follow the current product label and local agricultural recommendations.

severity_guidance.low: A small number of lesions on older leaves. Increase monitoring and improve cultural management.
severity_guidance.moderate: Multiple leaves affected with noticeable spread. Strengthen sanitation and consider locally registered disease-control measures.
severity_guidance.high: Extensive foliage infection or rapid defoliation. Seek local agricultural guidance and implement an integrated disease-management program.

farmer_advice: Keep potato plants healthy and monitor older leaves regularly. Early detection, crop rotation, good nutrition, and reducing prolonged leaf wetness can significantly improve disease management.
disclaimer: AI-based disease identification is decision support and not a definitive diagnosis. Confirm uncertain or severe cases with a qualified agricultural professional or plant diagnostic service.
